1.Rodolfo DuránRHBSD vs Emmet Sheehan (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.277 / xBA 0.233 / xSLG 0.377 (-38 pts)Park -19 pts14d —vs RHP —K 28.0%BB 5.9%HR 11.6%Hit 11.6%Slight Pitcher

Rodolfo Durán RHB vs Emmet Sheehan RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 51+0+0, pitcher rows 720+1304+0

Verdict

Sheehan's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Rodolfo Durán should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.277 xwOBA (-38 pts vs league, Pitcher). Emmet Sheehan is in a 14-day hot stretch (-63 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

3.Zach ColeLHBHOU vs Zebby Matthews (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.340 / xBA 0.285 / xSLG 0.482 (+25 pts)Park -19 pts14d 0.299vs RHP 0.324K 28.8%BB 5.5%HR 8.6%Hit 21.3%Slight Hitter

Zach Cole LHB vs Zebby Matthews RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 153+224+0, pitcher rows 83+1353+717

Verdict

Matthews' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach Cole should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.340 xwOBA (+25 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +30 pts (raw 0.329 -> adj 0.359); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

4.Bryce EldridgeLHBSF vs Ryne Nelson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.331 / xBA 0.244 / xSLG 0.436 (+16 pts)Park +6 pts14d 0.242vs RHP 0.251K 21.5%BB 7.1%HR 8.2%Hit 18.2%Even

Bryce Eldridge LHB vs Ryne Nelson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 112+160+0, pitcher rows 736+2467+2413

Verdict

Nelson's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Bryce Eldridge should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.331 xwOBA (+16 pts vs league, Hitter). Ryne Nelson is in a 14-day hot stretch (-104 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Verdict

Projection: 0.382 xwOBA (+67 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +24 pts (raw 0.355 -> adj 0.379); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Ben Brown is in a 14-day hot stretch (-60 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

6.Munetaka MurakamiLHBCWS vs Bryce Miller (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.319 / xBA 0.260 / xSLG 0.438 (+4 pts)Park -23 pts14d 0.338vs RHP 0.374K 23.0%BB 12.8%HR 7.6%Hit 15.5%Even

Munetaka Murakami LHB vs Bryce Miller RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 884+0+0, pitcher rows 85+1736+2753

Verdict

Miller's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Munetaka Murakami should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.319 xwOBA (+4 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.341 -> adj 0.334); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.334 -> final 0.319); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

7.Drew RomoLHBCWS vs Bryce Miller (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.315 / xBA 0.255 / xSLG 0.439 (-0 pts)Park -17 pts14d 0.259vs RHP 0.253K 20.4%BB 9.4%HR 7.5%Hit 17.2%Even

Drew Romo LHB vs Bryce Miller RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 185+12+189, pitcher rows 85+1736+2753

Verdict

Miller's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Drew Romo should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.315 xwOBA (-0 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: +13 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.332); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Drew Romo is on a 14-day slump (-98 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

8.Samuel BasalloLHBBAL vs Griffin Jax (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.292 / xBA 0.259 / xSLG 0.398 (-23 pts)Park -16 pts14d 0.397vs RHP 0.370K 28.1%BB 5.1%HR 6.9%Hit 17.9%Even

Samuel Basallo LHB vs Griffin Jax RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 501+443+0, pitcher rows 378+1105+1072

Verdict

Jax's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Samuel Basallo should look to drive the sweeper. Projection: 0.292 xwOBA (-23 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.314 -> final 0.292); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Samuel Basallo is on a 14-day heater (+57 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

9.Eric HaaseRHBSF vs Ryne Nelson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.335 / xBA 0.260 / xSLG 0.445 (+20 pts)Park +5 pts14d —vs RHP —K 27.7%BB 4.4%HR 6.9%Hit 22.8%Even

Eric Haase RHB vs Ryne Nelson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 93+339+270, pitcher rows 736+2467+2413

Verdict

Nelson's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Eric Haase should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.335 xwOBA (+20 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +18 pts (raw 0.312 -> adj 0.330); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Ryne Nelson is in a 14-day hot stretch (-104 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 69.5 mph
swing length 7.0 ft
attack angle 11.2°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.7+7.731+3.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

10.Braden ShewmakeLHBHOU vs Zebby Matthews (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.335 / xBA 0.280 / xSLG 0.482 (+20 pts)Park -20 pts14d 0.260vs RHP 0.289K 22.5%BB 5.0%HR 6.8%Hit 24.0%Even

Braden Shewmake LHB vs Zebby Matthews RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 166+0+214, pitcher rows 83+1353+717

Verdict

Matthews' slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Braden Shewmake should look to drive the changeup. Projection: 0.335 xwOBA (+20 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +35 pts (raw 0.320 -> adj 0.355); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

11.Jesus RodriguezRHBSF vs Ryne Nelson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.332 / xBA 0.256 / xSLG 0.435 (+17 pts)Park +7 pts14d 0.299vs RHP —K 21.5%BB 5.2%HR 6.6%Hit 24.0%Even

Jesus Rodriguez RHB vs Ryne Nelson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 111+0+0, pitcher rows 736+2467+2413

Verdict

Nelson's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Jesus Rodriguez should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.332 xwOBA (+17 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +14 pts (raw 0.311 -> adj 0.325); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Ryne Nelson is in a 14-day hot stretch (-104 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 65.5 mph
swing length 7.1 ft
attack angle 5.3°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.7+3.711-1.0on plane
Slider-8.3+5.3*4-3.0on plane
Cutter-6.6+5.3*2-1.3on plane
Curveball-9.9+5.3*0-4.6swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Sinker-4.8+1.210-3.6swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Sweeper-8.3+8.39-0.0on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

13.Jo AdellRHBLAA vs Jacob Lopez (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.300 / xBA 0.227 / xSLG 0.439 (-15 pts)Park -10 pts14d 0.383vs LHP —K 23.8%BB 9.3%HR 6.3%Hit 16.1%Even

Jo Adell RHB vs Jacob Lopez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 753+2221+1845, pitcher rows 749+1666+178

Verdict

Lopez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jo Adell should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.300 xwOBA (-15 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.320 -> final 0.300); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jo Adell is on a 14-day heater (+61 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Matthews' slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Brice Matthews should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.303 xwOBA (-12 pts vs league, Pitcher). 15.Randal GrichukRHBCWS vs Bryce Miller (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.318 / xBA 0.263 / xSLG 0.457 (+3 pts)Park -17 pts14d —vs RHP —K 18.8%BB 3.8%HR 6.1%Hit 22.3%Even

Randal Grichuk RHB vs Bryce Miller RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 197+1081+1091, pitcher rows 85+1736+2753

Verdict

Miller's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Randal Grichuk should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.318 xwOBA (+3 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: +15 pts (raw 0.321 -> adj 0.335); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

16.Jazz Chisholm Jr.LHBNYY vs Dylan Cease (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.340 / xBA 0.230 / xSLG 0.407 (+25 pts)Park +11 pts14d 0.274vs RHP 0.345K 31.2%BB 12.6%HR 6.1%Hit 19.2%Even

Jazz Chisholm Jr. LHB vs Dylan Cease RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 754+2253+2760, pitcher rows 904+3125+3308

Verdict

Cease's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Jazz Chisholm Jr. should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.340 xwOBA (+25 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.323 -> final 0.340); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

17.Kyle HigashiokaRHBTEX vs Sammy Peralta (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.305 / xBA 0.243 / xSLG 0.391 (-10 pts)Park +3 pts14d —vs LHP —K 9.1%BB 4.0%HR 6.1%Hit 21.7%Even

Kyle Higashioka RHB vs Sammy Peralta LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 307+1280+1143, pitcher rows 0+165+283

Verdict

Peralta's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Kyle Higashioka should look to drive the sweeper. Projection: 0.305 xwOBA (-10 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -16 pts (raw 0.320 -> adj 0.304); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

18.Aaron JudgeRHBNYY vs Dylan Cease (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.346 / xBA 0.241 / xSLG 0.430 (+31 pts)Park +10 pts14d 0.345vs RHP 0.327K 30.8%BB 11.7%HR 6.0%Hit 20.5%Slight Hitter

Aaron Judge RHB vs Dylan Cease RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 910+2875+3235, pitcher rows 904+3125+3308

Verdict

Cease's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Aaron Judge should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.346 xwOBA (+31 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -29 pts (raw 0.353 -> adj 0.323); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.323 -> final 0.346); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Aaron Judge is on a 14-day slump (-84 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

19.Rafael DeversLHBSF vs Ryne Nelson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.356 / xBA 0.258 / xSLG 0.467 (+41 pts)Park +4 pts14d 0.272vs RHP 0.298K 21.4%BB 9.0%HR 5.8%Hit 23.4%Slight Hitter

Rafael Devers LHB vs Ryne Nelson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 800+3102+2433, pitcher rows 736+2467+2413

Verdict

Nelson's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Rafael Devers should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.356 xwOBA (+41 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.341 -> adj 0.347); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.347 -> final 0.356); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ryne Nelson is in a 14-day hot stretch (-104 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 69.3 mph
swing length 7.7 ft
attack angle 11.6°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sweeper-7.2+20.316+13.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-5.4+2.319-3.1swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
4-Seam Fastball-4.4+4.320-0.2on plane
Cutter-6.1+11.98+5.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Changeup-7.7+11.6*4+3.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

21.Pete Crow-ArmstrongLHBCHC vs Jacob Misiorowski (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.335 / xBA 0.255 / xSLG 0.419 (+20 pts)14d 0.332vs RHP 0.314K 38.1%BB 6.9%HR 5.6%Hit 22.0%Even

Pete Crow-Armstrong LHB vs Jacob Misiorowski RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 704+2403+1424, pitcher rows 733+1349+0

Verdict

Misiorowski's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.335 xwOBA (+20 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +30 pts (raw 0.299 -> adj 0.329); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.329 -> final 0.335); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jacob Misiorowski is in a 14-day hot stretch (-61 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Projection: 0.330 xwOBA (+15 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +5 pts (raw 0.320 -> adj 0.326); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. 24.Brandon LoweLHBPIT vs Matthew Liberatore (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.289 / xBA 0.251 / xSLG 0.410 (-26 pts)Park -6 pts14d 0.411vs LHP —K 25.1%BB 5.5%HR 5.6%Hit 19.8%Slight Pitcher

Brandon Lowe LHB vs Matthew Liberatore LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 751+2148+1632, pitcher rows 685+2390+1370

Verdict

Liberatore's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Brandon Lowe should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.289 xwOBA (-26 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +10 pts (raw 0.304 -> adj 0.314); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.314 -> final 0.289); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

25.Casey SchmittRHBSF vs Ryne Nelson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.338 / xBA 0.277 / xSLG 0.432 (+23 pts)Park +4 pts14d 0.313vs RHP 0.349K 23.6%BB 4.4%HR 5.5%Hit 25.0%Even

Casey Schmitt RHB vs Ryne Nelson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 560+1336+374, pitcher rows 736+2467+2413

Verdict

Nelson's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Casey Schmitt should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.338 xwOBA (+23 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +11 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.330); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Casey Schmitt is on a 14-day slump (-44 pts vs season). Ryne Nelson is in a 14-day hot stretch (-104 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

26.Davis SchneiderRHBTOR vs Will Warren (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.322 / xBA 0.262 / xSLG 0.419 (+7 pts)Park +15 pts14d —vs RHP —K 32.2%BB 7.6%HR 5.5%Hit 21.3%Even

Davis Schneider RHB vs Will Warren RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 361+1115+1975, pitcher rows 802+2892+458

Verdict

Warren's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Davis Schneider should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.322 xwOBA (+7 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +19 pts (raw 0.312 -> adj 0.331); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -24 pts (bb_type-adj 0.331 -> final 0.322); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

27.Junior CamineroRHBTB vs Kyle Bradish (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.311 / xBA 0.245 / xSLG 0.417 (-4 pts)Park -17 pts14d 0.414vs RHP 0.379K 27.8%BB 6.3%HR 5.5%Hit 21.3%Even

Junior Caminero RHB vs Kyle Bradish RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 759+2498+637, pitcher rows 835+494+674

Verdict

Bradish's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Junior Caminero should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.311 xwOBA (-4 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: +5 pts (raw 0.312 -> adj 0.317); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.317 -> final 0.311); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Junior Caminero is on a 14-day heater (+51 pts vs season). Kyle Bradish is in a 14-day hot stretch (-118 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

28.Jake BurgerRHBTEX vs Sammy Peralta (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.352 / xBA 0.267 / xSLG 0.423 (+37 pts)Park +8 pts14d 0.356vs LHP —K 9.1%BB 4.4%HR 5.5%Hit 27.4%Slight Hitter

Jake Burger RHB vs Sammy Peralta LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 660+1324+2089, pitcher rows 0+165+283

Verdict

Peralta's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jake Burger should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.352 xwOBA (+37 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +13 pts (raw 0.317 -> adj 0.329); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +15 pts (bb_type-adj 0.329 -> final 0.352); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jake Burger is on a 14-day heater (+73 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Misiorowski's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Michael Busch should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.351 xwOBA (+36 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +17 pts (raw 0.332 -> adj 0.348); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Michael Busch is on a 14-day heater (+129 pts vs season). Jacob Misiorowski is in a 14-day hot stretch (-61 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Knuckle Curve10.50.3170.319GB 51% / LD 28% / FB 16% / PU 5%0.290-260.2950.2500.34651.9

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 75.4 mph
swing length 7.8 ft
attack angle 11.6°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.2+4.3123+0.1on plane
Split-Finger-7.4+15.720+8.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Knuckle Curve-9.4+18.811+9.5swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-5.6+6.223+0.7on plane
Slider-7.1+18.741+11.6swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sweeper-8.0+20.513+12.5swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-4.3+13.035+8.6swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

32.Esteury RuizRHBMIA vs Martín Pérez (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.319 / xBA 0.251 / xSLG 0.435 (+4 pts)Park -11 pts14d —vs LHP —K 20.2%BB 9.0%HR 5.4%Hit 21.5%Even

Esteury Ruiz RHB vs Martín Pérez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 106+88+246, pitcher rows 555+904+2208

Verdict

Pérez's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Esteury Ruiz should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.319 xwOBA (+4 pts vs league, Even). Martín Pérez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-56 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

33.Shohei OhtaniLHBLAD vs Griffin Canning (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.345 / xBA 0.256 / xSLG 0.483 (+30 pts)Park -22 pts14d 0.376vs RHP 0.434K 22.0%BB 16.2%HR 5.4%Hit 17.3%Slight Hitter

Shohei Ohtani LHB vs Griffin Canning RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 792+3241+3152, pitcher rows 220+1343+2876

Verdict

Projection: 0.345 xwOBA (+30 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -31 pts (raw 0.380 -> adj 0.349); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +19 pts (bb_type-adj 0.349 -> final 0.345); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

4-Seam Fastball45.10.3290.320GB 38% / LD 23% / FB 29% / PU 10%0.325-40.3300.2430.38211.0
Slider32.60.3060.282GB 36% / LD 25% / FB 32% / PU 7%0.325+190.2920.2380.36136.8
Knuckle Curve12.80.3040.320GB 46% / LD 21% / FB 33% / PU 0%0.309+50.3140.2510.41658.0

Cutter0.30.3170.315GB 38% / LD 23% / FB 30% / PU 9%0.328+120.3280.2600.4100.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

35.Daulton VarshoLHBTOR vs Will Warren (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.340 / xBA 0.245 / xSLG 0.412 (+25 pts)Park +11 pts14d 0.347vs RHP 0.356K 23.7%BB 9.3%HR 5.2%Hit 21.6%Even

Daulton Varsho LHB vs Will Warren RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 652+1298+2054, pitcher rows 802+2892+458

Verdict

Warren's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Daulton Varsho should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.340 xwOBA (+25 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.322 -> final 0.340); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

36.Henry DavisRHBPIT vs Matthew Liberatore (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.355 / xBA 0.299 / xSLG 0.500 (+40 pts)Park -15 pts14d —vs LHP —K 18.5%BB 6.3%HR 5.2%Hit 25.6%Slight Hitter

Henry Davis RHB vs Matthew Liberatore LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 377+1070+522, pitcher rows 685+2390+1370

Verdict

Liberatore's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Henry Davis should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.355 xwOBA (+40 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +44 pts (raw 0.327 -> adj 0.371); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

38.Trent GrishamLHBNYY vs Dylan Cease (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.340 / xBA 0.240 / xSLG 0.423 (+25 pts)Park +10 pts14d 0.250vs RHP 0.256K 27.5%BB 16.1%HR 5.1%Hit 16.9%Even

Trent Grisham LHB vs Dylan Cease RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 718+2529+857, pitcher rows 904+3125+3308

Verdict

Cease's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Trent Grisham should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.340 xwOBA (+25 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +21 pts (raw 0.318 -> adj 0.338); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.338 -> final 0.340); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Trent Grisham is on a 14-day slump (-82 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

39.Willi CastroLHBCOL vs Tyler Alexander (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.311 / xBA 0.226 / xSLG 0.406 (-4 pts)Park +4 pts14d 0.250vs LHP —K 18.4%BB 2.9%HR 5.0%Hit 23.3%Even

Willi Castro LHB vs Tyler Alexander LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 565+1740+2511, pitcher rows 329+1603+1673

Verdict

Alexander's sinker is the projected matchup advantage, while Willi Castro should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.311 xwOBA (-4 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.306 -> adj 0.297); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.297 -> final 0.311); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

40.Julio RodríguezRHBSEA vs Anthony Kay (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.319 / xBA 0.258 / xSLG 0.424 (+4 pts)Park -14 pts14d 0.320vs LHP —K 12.4%BB 9.6%HR 4.9%Hit 20.0%Even

Julio Rodríguez RHB vs Anthony Kay LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 828+2727+2389, pitcher rows 792+0+0

Verdict

Kay's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Julio Rodríguez should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.319 xwOBA (+4 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.342 -> adj 0.325); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.325 -> final 0.319); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Anthony Kay is in a 14-day hot stretch (-99 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

41.Ben RiceLHBNYY vs Dylan Cease (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.354 / xBA 0.244 / xSLG 0.446 (+39 pts)Park +9 pts14d 0.316vs RHP 0.362K 25.9%BB 11.9%HR 4.9%Hit 21.2%Slight Hitter

Ben Rice LHB vs Dylan Cease RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 739+2129+725, pitcher rows 904+3125+3308

Verdict

Cease's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Ben Rice should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.354 xwOBA (+39 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.345 -> adj 0.338); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.338 -> final 0.354); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ben Rice is on a 14-day slump (-99 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.311 -> adj 0.298); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

44.Matt ChapmanRHBSF vs Ryne Nelson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.353 / xBA 0.256 / xSLG 0.443 (+38 pts)Park +3 pts14d 0.207vs RHP 0.208K 25.3%BB 7.1%HR 4.6%Hit 24.5%Slight Hitter

Matt Chapman RHB vs Ryne Nelson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 775+2213+2722, pitcher rows 736+2467+2413

Verdict

Nelson's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Matt Chapman should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.353 xwOBA (+38 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +23 pts (raw 0.315 -> adj 0.338); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.338 -> final 0.353); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ryne Nelson is in a 14-day hot stretch (-104 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

45.Carson KellyRHBCHC vs Jacob Misiorowski (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.306 / xBA 0.244 / xSLG 0.378 (-9 pts)Park -4 pts14d 0.289vs RHP —K 25.3%BB 8.0%HR 4.5%Hit 20.6%Even

Carson Kelly RHB vs Jacob Misiorowski RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 476+1770+1174, pitcher rows 733+1349+0

Verdict

Misiorowski's slider is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.306 xwOBA (-9 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +24 pts (raw 0.286 -> adj 0.309); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Jacob Misiorowski is in a 14-day hot stretch (-61 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball54.70.3080.309GB 38% / LD 25% / FB 29% / PU 8%0.325+170.3190.2320.37614.7
Slider33.80.2750.293GB 33% / LD 38% / FB 23% / PU 7%0.322+470.3000.2620.38731.4

Changeup0.30.3140.290GB 39% / LD 30% / FB 24% / PU 7%0.328+140.3040.2400.37622.6

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Verdict

Canning's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Teoscar Hernández should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.313 xwOBA (-2 pts vs league, Even). Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.323 -> final 0.313); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Cutter28.60.3180.330GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.318+00.3340.2560.44913.9

Curveball8.70.3160.323GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.316+00.3240.2500.42710.5
Split-Finger2.90.3080.313GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.308+00.3060.2400.39825.4

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

48.Andrew VaughnRHBMIL vs Ben Brown (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.306 / xBA 0.255 / xSLG 0.389 (-9 pts)Park -4 pts14d 0.447vs RHP —K 21.7%BB 4.1%HR 4.5%Hit 22.9%Even

Andrew Vaughn RHB vs Ben Brown RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 140+1834+2338, pitcher rows 463+1737+849

Verdict

Brown's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Andrew Vaughn should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.306 xwOBA (-9 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -23 pts (raw 0.329 -> adj 0.305); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Ben Brown is in a 14-day hot stretch (-60 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

49.Bo BichetteRHBNYM vs Foster Griffin (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.303 / xBA 0.221 / xSLG 0.390 (-12 pts)Park +18 pts14d 0.359vs LHP —K 16.1%BB 7.6%HR 4.5%Hit 20.9%Even

Bo Bichette RHB vs Foster Griffin LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 740+2433+1193, pitcher rows 834+0+0

Verdict

Griffin's sinker is the projected matchup advantage, while Bo Bichette should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.303 xwOBA (-12 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -33 pts (raw 0.328 -> adj 0.295); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.295 -> final 0.303); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Bryce Harper LHB vs Chase Burns RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 750+2264+2593, pitcher rows 814+778+0

Verdict

Burns' slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Bryce Harper should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.305 xwOBA (-10 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -45 pts (raw 0.344 -> adj 0.299); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

51.Drew GilbertLHBSF vs Ryne Nelson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.342 / xBA 0.248 / xSLG 0.470 (+27 pts)Park +3 pts14d 0.236vs RHP 0.278K 17.1%BB 5.4%HR 4.5%Hit 25.7%Slight Hitter

Drew Gilbert LHB vs Ryne Nelson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 351+416+0, pitcher rows 736+2467+2413

Verdict

Nelson's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Drew Gilbert should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.342 xwOBA (+27 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +22 pts (raw 0.316 -> adj 0.338); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Ryne Nelson is in a 14-day hot stretch (-104 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

53.Nolan GormanLHBSTL vs Mitch Keller (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.331 / xBA 0.264 / xSLG 0.417 (+16 pts)Park -8 pts14d 0.377vs RHP 0.381K 26.4%BB 8.6%HR 4.4%Hit 23.0%Even

Nolan Gorman LHB vs Mitch Keller RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 687+1663+1601, pitcher rows 781+2871+2852

Verdict

Keller's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Nolan Gorman should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.331 xwOBA (+16 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -11 pts (raw 0.340 -> adj 0.329); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.329 -> final 0.331); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Nolan Gorman is on a 14-day heater (+64 pts vs season). Mitch Keller is in a 14-day rough patch (+65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

54.Kyle ManzardoLHBCLE vs Keider Montero (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.355 / xBA 0.255 / xSLG 0.449 (+40 pts)Park +3 pts14d 0.458vs RHP 0.427K 18.1%BB 9.8%HR 4.4%Hit 24.1%Slight Hitter

Kyle Manzardo LHB vs Keider Montero RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 599+2074+709, pitcher rows 626+1549+1571

Verdict

Montero's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Kyle Manzardo should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.355 xwOBA (+40 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.336 -> final 0.355); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kyle Manzardo is on a 14-day heater (+133 pts vs season). Keider Montero is in a 14-day rough patch (+88 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

55.Lenyn SosaRHBTOR vs Will Warren (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.351 / xBA 0.264 / xSLG 0.447 (+36 pts)Park +21 pts14d 0.209vs RHP —K 28.7%BB 2.7%HR 4.4%Hit 29.8%Slight Hitter

Lenyn Sosa RHB vs Will Warren RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 354+2056+1384, pitcher rows 802+2892+458

Verdict

Warren's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Lenyn Sosa should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.351 xwOBA (+36 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +18 pts (raw 0.312 -> adj 0.330); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Lenyn Sosa is on a 14-day slump (-41 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

56.Cedric MullinsLHBTB vs Kyle Bradish (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.273 / xBA 0.228 / xSLG 0.351 (-42 pts)Park -17 pts14d 0.222vs RHP 0.260K 28.9%BB 12.5%HR 4.4%Hit 13.5%Slight Pitcher

Cedric Mullins LHB vs Kyle Bradish RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 578+2113+1958, pitcher rows 835+494+674

Verdict

Bradish's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Cedric Mullins should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.273 xwOBA (-42 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.302 -> adj 0.293); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Kyle Bradish is in a 14-day hot stretch (-118 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Curveball27.20.3080.292GB 52% / LD 32% / FB 7% / PU 9%0.288-200.2650.2220.31946.9
Sinker27.20.3090.317GB 50% / LD 17% / FB 24% / PU 9%0.300-90.3020.2180.3754.9

Slider19.50.3040.284GB 54% / LD 12% / FB 22% / PU 12%0.275-290.2440.1850.25630.6

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

58.James WoodLHBWSH vs Nolan McLean (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.371 / xBA 0.253 / xSLG 0.436 (+56 pts)Park +21 pts14d 0.434vs RHP 0.468K 34.1%BB 14.7%HR 4.4%Hit 21.4%Edge Hitter

James Wood LHB vs Nolan McLean RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 961+2923+1406, pitcher rows 833+740+0

Verdict

McLean's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while James Wood should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.371 xwOBA (+56 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.337 -> final 0.371); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Nolan McLean is in a 14-day rough patch (+66 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

59.Luke RaleyLHBSEA vs Anthony Kay (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.303 / xBA 0.242 / xSLG 0.424 (-12 pts)Park -18 pts14d 0.427vs LHP —K 29.8%BB 1.2%HR 4.3%Hit 19.3%Even

Luke Raley LHB vs Anthony Kay LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 509+850+1714, pitcher rows 792+0+0

Verdict

Kay's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.303 xwOBA (-12 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +18 pts (raw 0.304 -> adj 0.321); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Anthony Kay is in a 14-day hot stretch (-99 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

60.Vladimir Guerrero Jr.RHBTOR vs Will Warren (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.385 / xBA 0.277 / xSLG 0.503 (+70 pts)Park +15 pts14d 0.304vs RHP 0.321K 20.9%BB 6.1%HR 4.3%Hit 29.6%Edge Hitter

Vladimir Guerrero Jr. RHB vs Will Warren RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 745+2875+2643, pitcher rows 802+2892+458

Verdict

Warren's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Vladimir Guerrero Jr. should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.385 xwOBA (+70 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +20 pts (raw 0.347 -> adj 0.367); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Vladimir Guerrero Jr. is on a 14-day slump (-49 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sinker39.90.3460.323GB 56% / LD 28% / FB 14% / PU 2%0.363+180.3720.3040.4734.8

Cutter0.20.3520.307GB 41% / LD 28% / FB 27% / PU 4%0.360+80.3530.2730.48526.1

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Ian Happ LHB vs Jacob Misiorowski RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 863+2839+2728, pitcher rows 733+1349+0

Verdict

Misiorowski's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Ian Happ should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.341 xwOBA (+26 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +15 pts (raw 0.321 -> adj 0.336); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Jacob Misiorowski is in a 14-day hot stretch (-61 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

62.Juan SotoLHBNYM vs Foster Griffin (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.353 / xBA 0.263 / xSLG 0.434 (+38 pts)Park +25 pts14d 0.277vs LHP —K 24.1%BB 12.8%HR 4.3%Hit 21.9%Slight Hitter

Juan Soto LHB vs Foster Griffin LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 523+3015+3255, pitcher rows 834+0+0

Verdict

Griffin's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Juan Soto should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.353 xwOBA (+38 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: -11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.339 -> final 0.353); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Juan Soto is on a 14-day slump (-86 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Cutter31.20.3260.330GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.341+150.3560.2790.47031.3

Split-Finger2.50.3130.313GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.323+100.3210.2560.41227.1

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

63.Dylan CrewsRHBWSH vs Nolan McLean (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.302 / xBA 0.239 / xSLG 0.358 (-13 pts)Park +17 pts14d —vs RHP —K 30.7%BB 4.8%HR 4.2%Hit 22.2%Even

Dylan Crews RHB vs Nolan McLean RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 0+1150+521, pitcher rows 833+740+0

Verdict

McLean's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Dylan Crews should look to drive the sweeper. Projection: 0.302 xwOBA (-13 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -20 pts (raw 0.314 -> adj 0.293); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.293 -> final 0.302); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Nolan McLean is in a 14-day rough patch (+66 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

65.Miguel VargasRHBCWS vs Bryce Miller (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.294 / xBA 0.241 / xSLG 0.411 (-21 pts)Park -20 pts14d 0.493vs RHP 0.467K 18.1%BB 6.5%HR 4.1%Hit 19.4%Even

Miguel Vargas RHB vs Bryce Miller RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 800+2326+1009, pitcher rows 85+1736+2753

Verdict

Miller's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Miguel Vargas should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.294 xwOBA (-21 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.331 -> adj 0.316); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Miguel Vargas is on a 14-day heater (+91 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

66.Marcell OzunaRHBPIT vs Matthew Liberatore (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.315 / xBA 0.265 / xSLG 0.471 (+0 pts)Park -9 pts14d 0.325vs LHP —K 20.3%BB 11.0%HR 4.0%Hit 19.9%Even

Marcell Ozuna RHB vs Matthew Liberatore LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 710+2643+2914, pitcher rows 685+2390+1370

Verdict

Liberatore's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Marcell Ozuna should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.315 xwOBA (+0 pts vs league, Even). Count-state blend: -22 pts (bb_type-adj 0.346 -> final 0.315); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

67.Willson ContrerasRHBBOS vs Bailey Falter (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.323 / xBA 0.249 / xSLG 0.438 (+8 pts)Park -30 pts14d 0.341vs LHP —K 16.3%BB 12.1%HR 4.0%Hit 19.3%Even

Willson Contreras RHB vs Bailey Falter LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 715+2163+1391, pitcher rows 96+1952+2140

Verdict

Falter's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Willson Contreras should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.323 xwOBA (+8 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.343 -> adj 0.330); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +23 pts (bb_type-adj 0.330 -> final 0.323); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Willson Contreras is on a 14-day slump (-53 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

68.Nolan ArenadoRHBAZ vs Landen Roupp (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.325 / xBA 0.260 / xSLG 0.401 (+10 pts)Park +5 pts14d 0.417vs RHP 0.426K 20.2%BB 6.0%HR 4.0%Hit 24.3%Even

Nolan Arenado RHB vs Landen Roupp RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 670+1603+2463, pitcher rows 859+1853+907

Verdict

Roupp's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Nolan Arenado should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.325 xwOBA (+10 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.311 -> adj 0.317); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Nolan Arenado is on a 14-day heater (+78 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 842+2018+2437, pitcher rows 733+1349+0

Verdict

Misiorowski's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Alex Bregman should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.319 xwOBA (+4 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: +14 pts (raw 0.308 -> adj 0.321); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Jacob Misiorowski is in a 14-day hot stretch (-61 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball55.00.3350.309GB 38% / LD 25% / FB 29% / PU 8%0.341+60.3350.2490.4159.1
Slider33.60.3010.293GB 33% / LD 38% / FB 23% / PU 7%0.336+350.3150.2710.40830.0
Curveball11.10.3030.301GB 55% / LD 22% / FB 15% / PU 8%0.289-150.2740.2320.3258.7
Changeup0.30.3240.290GB 39% / LD 30% / FB 24% / PU 7%0.321-30.2970.2210.38315.3

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

70.William ContrerasRHBMIL vs Ben Brown (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.317 / xBA 0.259 / xSLG 0.419 (+2 pts)Park -7 pts14d 0.296vs RHP 0.329K 18.9%BB 6.7%HR 3.9%Hit 23.3%Even

William Contreras RHB vs Ben Brown RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 666+2710+2744, pitcher rows 463+1737+849

Verdict

Brown's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while William Contreras should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.317 xwOBA (+2 pts vs league, Even). Ben Brown is in a 14-day hot stretch (-60 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.
