71.CJ AbramsLHBWSH vs Nolan McLean (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.365 / xBA 0.276 / xSLG 0.419 (+50 pts)Park +20 pts14d 0.270vs RHP 0.276K 24.4%BB 8.6%HR 3.9%Hit 24.0%Edge Hitter

CJ Abrams LHB vs Nolan McLean RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 769+2324+2273, pitcher rows 833+740+0

Verdict

McLean's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while CJ Abrams should look to drive the sweeper. Projection: 0.365 xwOBA (+50 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +16 pts (raw 0.320 -> adj 0.337); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.337 -> final 0.365); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. CJ Abrams is on a 14-day slump (-76 pts vs season). Nolan McLean is in a 14-day rough patch (+66 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

72.Tyler O'NeillRHBBAL vs Griffin Jax (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.274 / xBA 0.221 / xSLG 0.344 (-41 pts)Park -18 pts14d 0.265vs RHP —K 34.3%BB 9.8%HR 3.9%Hit 16.7%Slight Pitcher

Tyler O'Neill RHB vs Griffin Jax RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 344+827+2003, pitcher rows 378+1105+1072

Verdict

Jax's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Tyler O'Neill should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.274 xwOBA (-41 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -18 pts (raw 0.300 -> adj 0.282); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.282 -> final 0.274); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

74.Ryan McMahonLHBNYY vs Dylan Cease (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.315 / xBA 0.214 / xSLG 0.382 (-0 pts)Park +6 pts14d 0.209vs RHP —K 33.9%BB 12.7%HR 3.9%Hit 18.7%Even

Ryan McMahon LHB vs Dylan Cease RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 513+2572+2741, pitcher rows 904+3125+3308

Verdict

Cease's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Ryan McMahon should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.315 xwOBA (-0 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -18 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.300); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.300 -> final 0.315); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ryan McMahon is on a 14-day slump (-60 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

75.Fernando Tatis Jr.RHBSD vs Emmet Sheehan (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.310 / xBA 0.256 / xSLG 0.438 (-5 pts)Park -20 pts14d 0.310vs RHP 0.316K 25.8%BB 6.2%HR 3.9%Hit 22.8%Even

Fernando Tatis Jr. RHB vs Emmet Sheehan RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 721+2699+1628, pitcher rows 720+1304+0

Verdict

Sheehan's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Fernando Tatis Jr. should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.310 xwOBA (-5 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -5 pts (raw 0.335 -> adj 0.330); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Emmet Sheehan is in a 14-day hot stretch (-63 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

76.Spencer TorkelsonRHBDET vs Parker Messick (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.292 / xBA 0.259 / xSLG 0.347 (-23 pts)Park +9 pts14d 0.224vs LHP —K 27.0%BB 8.1%HR 3.9%Hit 19.3%Even

Spencer Torkelson RHB vs Parker Messick LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 788+2815+1716, pitcher rows 821+625+0

Verdict

Messick's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Spencer Torkelson should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.292 xwOBA (-23 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.299); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.299 -> final 0.292); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Spencer Torkelson is on a 14-day slump (-101 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

77.Seiya SuzukiRHBCHC vs Jacob Misiorowski (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.307 / xBA 0.241 / xSLG 0.373 (-8 pts)Park -4 pts14d 0.249vs RHP 0.243K 29.8%BB 8.8%HR 3.9%Hit 19.9%Even

Seiya Suzuki RHB vs Jacob Misiorowski RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 619+2841+2520, pitcher rows 733+1349+0

Verdict

Misiorowski's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Seiya Suzuki should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.307 xwOBA (-8 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.304 -> final 0.307); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Seiya Suzuki is on a 14-day slump (-85 pts vs season). Jacob Misiorowski is in a 14-day hot stretch (-61 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

79.Dansby SwansonRHBCHC vs Jacob Misiorowski (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.317 / xBA 0.246 / xSLG 0.401 (+2 pts)Park -6 pts14d 0.256vs RHP 0.279K 27.9%BB 8.2%HR 3.8%Hit 22.0%Even

Dansby Swanson RHB vs Jacob Misiorowski RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 725+2625+2365, pitcher rows 733+1349+0

Verdict

Misiorowski's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Dansby Swanson should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.317 xwOBA (+2 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.316); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.316 -> final 0.317); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Dansby Swanson is on a 14-day slump (-60 pts vs season). Jacob Misiorowski is in a 14-day hot stretch (-61 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

4-Seam Fastball54.90.3430.309GB 38% / LD 25% / FB 29% / PU 8%0.323-210.3160.2270.39925.9

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-3.8+11.6248+7.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Curveball-8.1+18.031+9.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Montero's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Chase DeLauter should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.400 xwOBA (+85 pts vs league, Hitter). Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

81.Edouard JulienLHBCOL vs Tyler Alexander (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.305 / xBA 0.230 / xSLG 0.396 (-10 pts)Park +10 pts14d 0.266vs LHP —K 21.3%BB 3.9%HR 3.8%Hit 22.4%Even

Edouard Julien LHB vs Tyler Alexander LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 564+876+1236, pitcher rows 329+1603+1673

Verdict

Alexander's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Edouard Julien should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.305 xwOBA (-10 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.303 -> adj 0.295); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Edouard Julien is on a 14-day slump (-64 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sweeper35.00.3040.293GB 32% / LD 20% / FB 38% / PU 10%0.303-10.2810.2270.38250.9

Slider0.10.3060.303GB 39% / LD 19% / FB 31% / PU 11%0.296-100.2840.2320.38430.6

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

83.Richie PalaciosLHBTB vs Kyle Bradish (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.278 / xBA 0.224 / xSLG 0.365 (-37 pts)Park -17 pts14d 0.272vs RHP 0.261K 27.9%BB 15.1%HR 3.8%Hit 12.3%Slight Pitcher

Richie Palacios LHB vs Kyle Bradish RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 360+190+1286, pitcher rows 835+494+674

Verdict

Bradish's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Richie Palacios should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.278 xwOBA (-37 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -11 pts (raw 0.313 -> adj 0.302); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.302 -> final 0.278); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Richie Palacios is on a 14-day slump (-57 pts vs season). Kyle Bradish is in a 14-day hot stretch (-118 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

84.Harrison BaderRHBSF vs Ryne Nelson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.343 / xBA 0.250 / xSLG 0.435 (+28 pts)Park +6 pts14d 0.326vs RHP —K 24.1%BB 4.5%HR 3.8%Hit 26.8%Slight Hitter

Harrison Bader RHB vs Ryne Nelson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 288+1961+1693, pitcher rows 736+2467+2413

Verdict

Nelson's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Harrison Bader should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.343 xwOBA (+28 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +18 pts (raw 0.309 -> adj 0.327); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.327 -> final 0.343); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ryne Nelson is in a 14-day hot stretch (-104 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

85.JJ WetherholtLHBSTL vs Mitch Keller (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.328 / xBA 0.285 / xSLG 0.448 (+13 pts)Park -16 pts14d 0.315vs RHP 0.310K 17.6%BB 7.2%HR 3.8%Hit 21.4%Even

JJ Wetherholt LHB vs Mitch Keller RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 827+0+0, pitcher rows 781+2871+2852

Verdict

Keller's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while JJ Wetherholt should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.328 xwOBA (+13 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: -6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.349 -> final 0.328); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Mitch Keller is in a 14-day rough patch (+65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

4-Seam Fastball39.50.3490.349GB 30% / LD 32% / FB 32% / PU 6%0.351+20.3850.3050.51114.1
Curveball12.70.3100.317GB 48% / LD 26% / FB 21% / PU 5%0.302-80.3040.2570.3680.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

86.Zach NetoRHBLAA vs Jacob Lopez (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.314 / xBA 0.222 / xSLG 0.406 (-1 pts)Park -9 pts14d 0.388vs LHP —K 25.4%BB 9.9%HR 3.8%Hit 19.8%Even

Zach Neto RHB vs Jacob Lopez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 877+2197+2295, pitcher rows 749+1666+178

Verdict

Lopez's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach Neto should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.314 xwOBA (-1 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.315 -> adj 0.306); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +17 pts (bb_type-adj 0.306 -> final 0.314); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Zach Neto is on a 14-day heater (+62 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

87.Pete AlonsoRHBBAL vs Griffin Jax (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.267 / xBA 0.214 / xSLG 0.342 (-48 pts)Park -16 pts14d 0.323vs RHP 0.314K 31.9%BB 9.1%HR 3.8%Hit 16.3%Slight Pitcher

Pete Alonso RHB vs Griffin Jax RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 833+2825+3049, pitcher rows 378+1105+1072

Verdict

Jax's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Pete Alonso should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.267 xwOBA (-48 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -49 pts (raw 0.322 -> adj 0.273); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.273 -> final 0.267); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

88.Jackson MerrillLHBSD vs Emmet Sheehan (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.304 / xBA 0.231 / xSLG 0.425 (-11 pts)Park -21 pts14d 0.278vs RHP 0.282K 27.3%BB 7.1%HR 3.7%Hit 20.6%Even

Jackson Merrill LHB vs Emmet Sheehan RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 748+1748+2291, pitcher rows 720+1304+0

Verdict

Sheehan's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jackson Merrill should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.304 xwOBA (-11 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -26 pts (raw 0.339 -> adj 0.313); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.313 -> final 0.304); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Emmet Sheehan is in a 14-day hot stretch (-63 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 90.Oneil CruzLHBPIT vs Matthew Liberatore (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.303 / xBA 0.238 / xSLG 0.430 (-12 pts)Park -7 pts14d 0.304vs LHP —K 27.9%BB 7.2%HR 3.7%Hit 21.6%Even

Oneil Cruz LHB vs Matthew Liberatore LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 819+2250+2404, pitcher rows 685+2390+1370

Verdict

Liberatore's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Oneil Cruz should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.303 xwOBA (-12 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +11 pts (raw 0.307 -> adj 0.318); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.318 -> final 0.303); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

91.Andy PagesRHBLAD vs Griffin Canning (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.291 / xBA 0.245 / xSLG 0.387 (-24 pts)Park -18 pts14d 0.366vs RHP 0.392K 18.0%BB 6.1%HR 3.7%Hit 20.8%Even

Andy Pages RHB vs Griffin Canning RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 785+2543+1810, pitcher rows 220+1343+2876

Verdict

Canning's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Andy Pages should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.291 xwOBA (-24 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.322 -> adj 0.301); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.301 -> final 0.291); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

92.Austin HedgesRHBCLE vs Keider Montero (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.336 / xBA 0.251 / xSLG 0.394 (+21 pts)Park +20 pts14d —vs RHP —K 21.6%BB 5.5%HR 3.7%Hit 26.2%Even

Austin Hedges RHB vs Keider Montero RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 276+709+598, pitcher rows 626+1549+1571

Verdict

Montero's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Austin Hedges should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.336 xwOBA (+21 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +14 pts (raw 0.297 -> adj 0.311); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Keider Montero is in a 14-day rough patch (+88 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

bat speed 65.9 mph
swing length 7.2 ft
attack angle 9.9°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sinker-5.6+7.024+1.4on plane
Slider-7.8+11.527+3.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
4-Seam Fastball-4.8+10.157+5.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Knuckle Curve-9.9+9.9*2+0.0on plane
Changeup-6.9+9.9*4+3.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

93.Jahmai JonesRHBDET vs Parker Messick (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.290 / xBA 0.241 / xSLG 0.372 (-25 pts)Park +10 pts14d —vs LHP —K 28.2%BB 7.1%HR 3.7%Hit 20.2%Even

Jahmai Jones RHB vs Parker Messick LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 193+615+152, pitcher rows 821+625+0

Verdict

Messick's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jahmai Jones should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.290 xwOBA (-25 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.297); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.297 -> final 0.290); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

94.Corbin CarrollLHBAZ vs Landen Roupp (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.346 / xBA 0.265 / xSLG 0.391 (+31 pts)Park +6 pts14d 0.446vs RHP 0.478K 20.9%BB 16.9%HR 3.7%Hit 17.3%Slight Hitter

Corbin Carroll LHB vs Landen Roupp RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 720+2514+2649, pitcher rows 859+1853+907

Verdict

Roupp's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Corbin Carroll should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.346 xwOBA (+31 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.333 -> final 0.346); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Corbin Carroll is on a 14-day heater (+65 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Lopez's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Oswald Peraza should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.324 xwOBA (+9 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +31 pts (raw 0.302 -> adj 0.333); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

96.Trevor StoryRHBBOS vs Bailey Falter (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.273 / xBA 0.252 / xSLG 0.417 (-42 pts)Park -33 pts14d 0.210vs LHP —K 18.0%BB 6.9%HR 3.6%Hit 19.8%Slight Pitcher

Trevor Story RHB vs Bailey Falter LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 643+2518+381, pitcher rows 96+1952+2140

Verdict

Falter's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Trevor Story should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.273 xwOBA (-42 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.327 -> adj 0.320); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.320 -> final 0.273); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball53.30.3170.331GB 34% / LD 26% / FB 28% / PU 13%0.303-150.3190.2550.4108.3
Slider16.00.3140.313GB 36% / LD 24% / FB 33% / PU 7%0.310-30.3090.2360.39927.7
Curveball15.10.3020.319GB 37% / LD 25% / FB 30% / PU 9%0.303+20.3070.2350.41636.8
Sinker8.20.3260.336GB 29% / LD 31% / FB 27% / PU 13%0.337+110.3580.2830.4733.3
Split-Finger7.30.3130.335GB 53% / LD 21% / FB 26% / PU 0%0.314+10.3350.2630.45359.3
Slurve0.20.3080.337GB 35% / LD 26% / FB 29% / PU 11%0.308+10.3310.2640.46017.6

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 70.1 mph
swing length 7.4 ft
attack angle 11.1°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.6+2.877-1.8on plane
Slider-6.8+15.538+8.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Curveball-8.6+20.828+12.2swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-4.8-1.121-5.9swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Split-Finger-6.3+11.1*3+4.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

98.Manny MachadoRHBSD vs Emmet Sheehan (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.282 / xBA 0.236 / xSLG 0.389 (-33 pts)Park -18 pts14d 0.264vs RHP 0.267K 26.3%BB 4.7%HR 3.6%Hit 21.4%Slight Pitcher

Manny Machado RHB vs Emmet Sheehan RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 763+2714+2490, pitcher rows 720+1304+0

Verdict

Sheehan's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Manny Machado should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.314 -> adj 0.298); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Emmet Sheehan is in a 14-day hot stretch (-63 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

4-Seam Fastball45.20.3490.325GB 28% / LD 27% / FB 31% / PU 15%0.317-330.3270.2390.45120.2

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

99.Jackson ChourioRHBMIL vs Ben Brown (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.300 / xBA 0.245 / xSLG 0.378 (-15 pts)Park -8 pts14d 0.237vs RHP 0.230K 22.0%BB 3.5%HR 3.6%Hit 24.2%Even

Jackson Chourio RHB vs Ben Brown RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 228+2228+2163, pitcher rows 463+1737+849

Verdict

Brown's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Jackson Chourio should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.300 xwOBA (-15 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -5 pts (raw 0.303 -> adj 0.298); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.298 -> final 0.300); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jackson Chourio is on a 14-day slump (-75 pts vs season). Ben Brown is in a 14-day hot stretch (-60 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

100.Ceddanne RafaelaRHBBOS vs Bailey Falter (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.291 / xBA 0.255 / xSLG 0.420 (-24 pts)Park -33 pts14d 0.326vs LHP —K 17.6%BB 6.3%HR 3.6%Hit 20.9%Even

Ceddanne Rafaela RHB vs Bailey Falter LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 605+2180+2074, pitcher rows 96+1952+2140

Verdict

Falter's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Ceddanne Rafaela should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.291 xwOBA (-24 pts vs league, Pitcher). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball53.60.3090.331GB 34% / LD 26% / FB 28% / PU 13%0.295-130.3110.2440.38712.9
Slider15.90.3120.313GB 36% / LD 24% / FB 33% / PU 7%0.314+20.3120.2500.41533.3

Sinker8.20.3190.336GB 29% / LD 31% / FB 27% / PU 13%0.384+650.4040.3030.5656.8
Split-Finger7.20.3110.335GB 53% / LD 21% / FB 26% / PU 0%0.310-10.3300.2670.43620.1
Slurve0.20.3090.337GB 35% / LD 26% / FB 29% / PU 11%0.313+50.3350.2580.47519.4

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).
